tavily.answer: In 2025, Tanzania experienced significant international displacement due to conflict and violence, with 32.3 million people displaced globally, surpassing disaster-driven displacements for the first time. The country faced allegations of election abuse and human rights violations, leading to the denial of entry to a European Parliament delegation investigating these issues. Additionally, the United States imposed sanctions on Tanzania’s police chief for human rights violations. Meanwhile, Uganda closed its border with the Democratic Republic of the Congo due to an Ebola outbreak that has killed over 220 people across the two countries.
